{{Quote|I'm glad you said there'd be no problem, hot-shot…otherwise I might be worried.|Leia Organa|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 41">[[Star Wars (1977) 41|''Star Wars'' (1977) 41]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars''''' '''41''' is the forty-first issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] escapes from the downed [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeder]] just as the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] approaches him. Grabbing a harpoon gun, he runs underneath the behemoth and fires the harpoon into the AT-AT's underside, and climbs up the rope and slices a hatch open with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Throwing a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] inside the AT-AT, he succeeds in destroying the vehicle.

Back at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] personally leads a contingent of [[Cold assault stormtrooper|snowtroopers]] throughout the rebel headquarters. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] rushes [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] through the access corridors as [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] gunfire pummels the base. He gets them aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and takes off.

As the Falcon leaves [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], two [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] immediately give chase. Han maneuvers the Falcon expertly between them causing the star destroyers to sideswipe one another. A squad of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] exits one of the cruisers and gives chase. Han leads the TIE bombers into a congested [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]].

Back on Hoth, Rogue group regroups and makes preparations to meet at the rendezvous point. But Luke isn't following the rest of his team. Instead, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and he take Luke's [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]] towards the planet [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]].

Arriving there, Luke lands his X-wing in a thick, swampy bog, R2 chirps a warning to him as he exits the cockpit. A strange, small [[Yoda/Legends|creature]] watches the two from afar.

==Continuity==
This is Chapter Three of the six-part adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

This issue features a five page article, "Building an Empire: Marvel Style" by [[Archie Goodwin]] which explains the discrepancy between the way [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] looked in the [[The Marvel Comics Illustrated Version of Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back|paperback adaptation]] and the other versions. It also reveals that both [[Al Williamson]] and [[Carlos Garzón]] drew each of the covers of the adaptation.
